title: Quiz: QAOA for Portfolio Optimization
description: Test your understanding of QAOA algorithm and its application to portfolio problems

questions.question: What does QAOA stand for?
questions.explanation: QAOA stands for Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm, a hybrid quantum-classical algorithm for combinatorial optimization problems.

questions.question: What are the two main components that alternate in a QAOA circuit?
questions.explanation: QAOA alternates between the cost unitary (encoding the optimization objective) and the mixer unitary (enabling exploration of the solution space).

questions.question: How are the variational parameters (γ and β) in QAOA optimized?

options.text: Using a classical optimizer based on measured expectation values
They are fixed and don't need optimization

questions.explanation: QAOA is a hybrid algorithm where the quantum computer evaluates the cost function and a classical optimizer adjusts the parameters to minimize the expected cost.

questions.question: What is the initial state in a standard QAOA circuit?
questions.explanation: Standard QAOA starts with all qubits in the |+⟩ state (equal superposition), achieved by applying Hadamard gates to all qubits initially in |0⟩.

questions.question: Increasing QAOA depth (p) always improves solution quality on real quantum hardware.
questions.explanation: False. While theoretically higher depth can improve solutions, on real noisy hardware, deeper circuits accumulate more errors. There's often an optimal depth beyond which noise degrades performance.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the mixer Hamiltonian in QAOA?

options.text: To enable transitions between computational basis states

questions.explanation: The mixer Hamiltonian (typically X gates on all qubits) allows the quantum state to explore different solutions by enabling transitions between computational basis states.

questions.question: What is warm-starting in QAOA?

options.text: Running the quantum computer at higher temperature
Using a classical solution to bias the initial quantum state

questions.explanation: Warm-starting uses information from classical heuristics to initialize the quantum state closer to good solutions, potentially improving convergence.

questions.question: Which factors affect QAOA performance for portfolio optimization? (Select all that apply)

options.text: The color of the quantum computer

questions.explanation: QAOA performance depends on circuit depth (affecting solution quality), classical optimizer (affecting parameter convergence), and shot count (affecting cost estimation accuracy).
